body.title: [triage] The single rack health monitor EXISTS, is wired to the RETIRED IMAP transport, runs its own forbidden daemon, and is instantiated by NOBODY — while its 14 tests pass. Rack health is de-facto a property of the web server being up.
body.intention: I intend that every device can report its own status on its own, so that rack health survives the death of any single component — including the web server — and that there is exactly ONE home for that health, which a reader can find without knowing which device happens to host it. [intention_date: 2026-07-13]
body.description:
**RAISED BY AKIEN 2026-07-13:** "there is supposed to be a single health monitoring for all the
parts of the system. even if the web server is not up, if a device is up, it should be able to deliver its
status pages — the ones the web server would use."

**IT WAS BUILT. IT IS A CORPSE. AND ITS TESTS ARE GREEN.**
`unseen_university/devices/librarian/health_aggregator.py` — `HealthAggregator`, collects per-device
heartbeats, exposes `rack_health()`. Exactly the thing. And:
  * its constructor takes an **`IMAPServer`** — the **RETIRED** transport. CLAUDE.md: "IMAP was the OLD
    transport and is fully removed — any IMAP/IMAPServer mention anywhere in this repo is stale debt
    (T-imap-references-purge)." The rack's health monitor is plumbed into a transport that no longer exists.
  * it has **`run_forever()`** — its own daemon, which "⛔ ONE daemon structure — the Ground Loop" forbids.
  * **NOTHING instantiates it.** The only callers in the whole repo are `tests/test_health_aggregator.py`.
    `health_tools.set_aggregator()` guards a module-global `_aggregator = None` that is never set in a live
    process.
  * it lives **inside librarian**, not as rack infrastructure — so "rack health" is a thing one device owns.

**AND ITS 14 TESTS PASS.** This is the day's defect one more time, in a new costume: **a green suite on a
component wired to a dead transport and constructed by no one. A passing test is indistinguishable from a
live capability.** (Cousins: the intent extractor's crash wearing CP1's clothes; HealthMonitor calling a
saturated source healthy; `test_ollama_liveness` "fixing" a reachability probe by replacing it with a
different reachability probe.)

**MEANWHILE, health is de-facto served BY THE WEB SERVER**: `/health`, `/api/rack/health`,
`/api/comms/health`, `/api/granny/health` all live in `devices/web_server/server.py`. So rack health is a
property of the web server being up — the exact coupling Akien says must not exist. **When the web server
dies, the rack stops being able to say whether it is alive.** That is the failure mode where health matters
most.

**WHAT IS ALREADY RIGHT:** `BaseDevice.health()` is an `@abstractmethod` — every device HAS a health method,
and `uptime()`, `startup_errors()`, `logs()` alongside it. The contract exists. **What is missing is any way
to REACH it that does not go through the web server.**

**Scope boundary:** IN — (a) the aggregator moves off IMAP onto **PgBus** and off `run_forever()` onto a
**shim-started `ShimLoopThread`** driven by the Ground Loop; (b) it is instantiated in a live process, or it
is deleted — a health monitor nobody constructs is worse than none, because its green tests assert that we
have one; (c) every device can serve its OWN status independent of the web server (the web server becomes a
CONSUMER of device status, never its source); (d) ONE home for rack health, findable without knowing which
device hosts it. OUT — the content of any individual device's `health()`; the inference-source capability
probe (T-healthmonitor-probes-reachability-not-capability).

**Test plan / discriminating fixture:** with the web server **stopped**, a running device must still report
its status, and the aggregate must still be obtainable. A build that serves health only through the web
server passes today's tests and fails this one — which is the entire point. Plus: a test that FAILS if
`HealthAggregator` is constructed by nothing in a live process (the dead-code detector — the check that would
have caught this years ago).

**Completion criteria:**
  1. Kill the web server; a running device still reports its status; rack health is still obtainable.
  2. `HealthAggregator` is either LIVE (constructed in a real process, on PgBus, on a shim thread) or GONE.
     There is no third state, and "has passing tests" is not one.
  3. No `IMAPServer` reference remains in the health path.
  4. A test exists that goes red if the health path is instantiated by nobody.
